directives.directive: Whenever Claude Code collaboration is used in this repository, launch the normal visible Claude Code interactive window (`claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`) by default. Only allow non-interactive `claude -p`/print mode when the user explicitly requests background execution or machine-consumable output.

Do not defer collaboration corrections to 'next time'. Apply the repository Claude protocol in the current task: narrow task packet, visible window by default, state-JSON-driven waiting, and Codex final verification.

When Claude Code owns a bounded investigation lane, do not repeat the same file search in the main Codex thread. Read the state JSON on normal completion and only open the detailed report on exception or attention conditions.

notes.content:
NTwain real smoke harness for validating the production-style event path lives under `build/NtwainRealSmoke`. It references `E:\Work\scan_project\packages\NTwain.3.7.5\lib
et462\NTwain.dll`, targets x86 `net8.0-windows`, opens the installed `mbf Virtual TWAIN Scanner` from `C:\Windows	wain_32`, and verifies `TwainSession.TransferReady` plus `DataTransferred native=True`. Typical run: build `build\NtwainRealSmoke\NtwainRealSmoke.csproj`, start `tools\FakeScannerPipeServer in\Release
et10.0\mbfTwain.FakeScannerPipeServer.dll` with `--image build	est-assets\page1.bmp --scan 0 --scan-after-begin-delay-ms 500 --connections 120 --revision <n>`, set `MBF_TWAIN_FORCE_UI=1`, then run `build\NtwainRealSmoke in\Release
et8.0-windows\NtwainRealSmoke.exe`. This caught the TWAIN protocol gap where NTwain queries `DAT_XFERGROUP/MSG_GET` and `DAT_PENDINGXFERS/MSG_GET` after `MSG_XFERREADY`.

User preference: whenever Claude Code is used, the user expects to see the normal visible Claude chat window, not a hidden/background `claude -p` run, unless background execution was explicitly requested. Prefer token-efficient collaboration: narrow delegation, low-frequency polling of `.codex_delegate/state/*.json`, and avoid re-reading Claude's full output when status is `ok`.
